patch.value: Project-scope extension install writes ~69MB node_modules per workspace (peer pm-cli + transitive Sentry/OTel)

.agents/pm/extensions/pm-changelog/node_modules is 69MB: @unbrained/pm-cli 25MB (installed because runtimeDependencyInstallSpecs in src/cli/commands/extension/install-sources.ts:454-456 deliberately includes peerDependencies so SDK imports resolve inside the extension dir) plus its transitive @sentry 22MB + @opentelemetry 21MB. Gitignored, so no repo bloat, but every workspace that runs pm install npm:pm-changelog (including the changelog:pm CI/release path on every changelog check) pays the download+disk cost, and the nested pm-cli copy can version-skew against the host. Ideas: symlink/inject the running CLI's own install root to satisfy the peer instead of reinstalling it; or publish a slim SDK-only package for extensions to peer on; or npm install --omit=peer plus an explicit resolution shim for @unbrained/pm-cli. Needs care: PR#74 learning says package extensions rely on the resolvable peer for defineExtension imports.
